_comment: Genericised evaluation fixture for the semantic reduce step (issue #1). A distilled, NON-proprietary analogue of a real dogfood ledger: money-movement / order-lifecycle framing, no foreign symbols or paths. 13 raw findings that signature dedup (file + line-bucket + claim-class + severity) leaves as 13 distinct keys, but which a semantic reduce collapses to 5 canonical issues. `claim_class` doubles as the ground-truth concept id, so a group-by-claim_class reduce reproduces the intended clustering. It encodes the two shapes signature dedup misses: (a) `settlement-drop` — one concept raised by 4 personas across two files spanning NON_BLOCKING->UGLY; (b) `expiry-key` — a wrong-key bug split across the 10-line bucket boundary (1686/1687 in bucket 168, 1690 in bucket 169) and by severity.

findings.title: Settlement confirmations share one unnamespaced result dict on the shared client
findings.evidence: confirmation and quote responses collide on one dict

findings.title: Confirmation payload lacks the fields needed to build a LedgerEntry; no fee-report callback exists
findings.evidence: payload missing amount/fee; nothing constructs the entry

findings.title: Reconciliation never touches the in-memory ledger — balances never move from real settlement activity
findings.evidence: reconcile() computes but never writes to the Ledger

findings.title: Settlement confirmations never reach drain_confirmations() — all live settlements silently dropped
findings.evidence: callback appends to a queue nothing drains

findings.title: Expiry sweep cancels resting orders under the composite key, not the bare account used at submission
findings.evidence: keys built as acct+sym; submission used bare acct

findings.title: Expiry-path key derivation does not match the bare-account keys used everywhere else
findings.evidence: derivation diverges one line below the sweep

findings.title: Wrong-key expiry sweep orphans resting orders — permanent re-entry blackout
findings.evidence: orphaned orders never cancelled; account locked out

findings.title: 10s ack timeout treated as definitive rejection with no compensating cancel
findings.evidence: order already transmitted; no cancel on timeout

findings.title: Ack timeout may fire while the venue has accepted the order — resulting order untracked if it later settles
findings.evidence: timeout path drops the pending order id

findings.title: Naked entry: if every ladder placement fails, the entry transfer is left completely unrecorded
findings.evidence: entry recorded only after ladder succeeds

findings.title: Ladder placement failure leaves the already-submitted entry transfer untracked
findings.evidence: no record written on the failure branch

findings.title: Ladder failure leaves an unmanaged live transfer — guaranteed duplicate entry next cycle
Daily circuit-breaker session reset uses local date.today(), not the UTC used elsewhere
findings.evidence: reset boundary drifts by the local offset
